welcome.speaker: Mia
welcome.textTemplate: Welcome to LingoVille, {playerName}! Your new home is right there: Apartment 4B. Let's go check it out!

lost.speaker: Narrator
lost.text: 3 buildings?? Where's 4B?? Wait, there's a guard. Let's ask for help.

guard_hi.speaker: Security Guard
guard_hi.text: Hi! How can I help you?

attention.speaker: Narrator
attention.text: The guard greeted you. Say hi back before asking.

q1_prompt.speaker: Question
q1_prompt.text: The guard greeted you. How do you say hi back? Complete the sentence: blank, I'm looking for Apartment 4B. A, Hi. B, Bye. C, Good night.

q1_correct.speaker: Mia
q1_correct.text: Perfect! Hi, I'm looking for Apartment 4B. "Hi" is the most common everyday greeting. Hi means hi. Hello is a little more formal. Hey is very casual, usually with friends.

guard_q1_correct.speaker: Security Guard
guard_q1_correct.text: Of course! What do you need?

q1_wrong_goodbye.speaker: Mia
q1_wrong_goodbye.text: Wait, you just got here! "Bye" is for leaving. Try "Hi" to say hello.

q1_wrong_goodnight.speaker: Mia
q1_wrong_goodnight.text: It's not bedtime yet! "Good night" is for nighttime goodbyes. Use "Hi" here.

guard_wait.speaker: Security Guard
guard_wait.text: Take your time. What are you looking for?

q2_prompt.speaker: Question
q2_prompt.text: You want to ask where Apartment 4B is. Complete the sentence: blank Apartment 4B. A, Where is. B, What is. C, How is.

q2_correct.speaker: Mia
q2_correct.text: Perfect! Where is Apartment 4B? "Where is" plus a place asks about location. "Where is Apartment 4B?" means, 4B gongyu zai nali?

guard_q2_correct.speaker: Security Guard
guard_q2_correct.text: Apartment 4B? Sure! Let me show you...

guard_route.speaker: Security Guard
guard_route.text: Go straight, then turn right. 4B is on your right!

guard_turn_left.speaker: Security Guard
guard_turn_left.text: Turn right at the end of the hallway.

turn_left_prompt.speaker: Narrator
turn_left_prompt.text: Listen carefully. Where should you turn?

q3_correct.speaker: Mia
q3_correct.text: Perfect! "Turn right" means xiang you zhuan. Turn left, turn right, and go straight are the direction words you need.

guard_hallway.speaker: Security Guard
guard_hallway.text: It's just down the hallway. You can't miss it!

thank_prompt.speaker: Narrator
thank_prompt.text: He was so helpful. You should thank him.

q4_prompt.speaker: Question
q4_prompt.text: The guard helped you find Apartment 4B. What's the best way to thank him?

q4_correct.speaker: Mia
q4_correct.text: Perfect! Thank you so much means fei chang gan xie. Use so much when someone really helped you.

guard_welcome.speaker: Security Guard
guard_welcome.text: You're welcome, dear! Welcome to LingoVille!

almost_home.speaker: Narrator
almost_home.text: Almost there! 4B, your new home is right around the corner.

q2_wrong_what.speaker: Mia
q2_wrong_what.text: Close one! "Where is" is for places. "What is" is for things. I've saved this in your Mistake Book.

q2_wrong_how.speaker: Mia
q2_wrong_how.text: Almost! "How is" asks how something feels. For places, use "Where is". Saved to your Mistake Book.
